requirements.label: Pair through attended PIN
requirements.statement: The system shall bind Telegram control to exactly one allowed user through an explicit attended pairing PIN before accepting session-control messages.
requirements.rationale: A personal Telegram bridge must fail closed until the intended operator is paired, while first-time setup should not force mobile users to type a long high-entropy code during an attended local setup window.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: A setup or pairing test demonstrates that setup creates a four-digit PIN valid for five minutes, private-chat plain PIN text and /start PIN input can pair the first valid user, wrong or expired PINs do not pair, repeated failed PIN attempts are bounded for the setup window, pairing records exactly one allowed Telegram user/chat and clears the pending secret, and messages from other users do not become pi turns.

requirements.label: Reject unauthorized Telegram input
requirements.statement: The system shall reject Telegram updates from users or chats that are not authorized for the paired bridge.
requirements.rationale: Telegram is a powerful remote control surface for local agent sessions, so unauthorized updates must not reach pi as commands, files, turns, steering, or follow-ups.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show that unauthorized update authors receive no control path into connected sessions and cannot enqueue turns, stop runs, switch sessions, or upload files into pi.

requirements.label: Broker inside extension
requirements.statement: The system shall perform Telegram polling, routing, and client brokering inside connected pi extension processes without requiring a separate external daemon, relay server, or broker service.
requirements.rationale: The project owner explicitly rejected an extra daemon; the bridge must own brokering as part of the extension package.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Repository inspection confirms broker election, polling, IPC, and routing are implemented by the extension runtime and documented setup does not require installing or running any separate broker daemon or relay service.

requirements.label: Avoid inbound workstation endpoint
requirements.statement: The system shall use Telegram long polling for the normal bridge workflow without requiring an inbound network endpoint on the local workstation.
requirements.rationale: The intended user should not have to expose the local machine to inbound traffic to supervise local sessions from Telegram.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Inspection confirms the normal runtime removes webhooks before polling with getUpdates, does not start a public webhook server, and setup documentation requires only bot token configuration and local pi commands.

requirements.label: Register connected session route
requirements.statement: The system shall register a pi session with the active broker and create or reuse a Telegram route when /telegram-connect is run.
requirements.rationale: A session must become reachable from Telegram on demand without restarting the bot or other sessions.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: A connection workflow demonstrates that running /telegram-connect makes the current session visible and addressable from Telegram, and repeated registration reuses existing route state instead of creating duplicate routes.

requirements.label: Disconnect one session route
requirements.statement: The system shall unregister a pi session and remove its Telegram route on explicit disconnect without disconnecting unrelated sessions.
requirements.rationale: Remote reachability must be revocable per session while preserving other supervised sessions.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: A disconnect workflow demonstrates that /telegram-disconnect removes the selected session route or topic and leaves other connected sessions listed and reachable.

requirements.label: Deprecated offline route preservation
requirements.statement: The system shall mark a shutting-down session offline without deleting durable routes, pending turns, or retryable final responses that may still be recovered.
requirements.rationale: Deprecated 2026-04-25: this requirement preserved Telegram routes for ordinary shutdown, but the project owner clarified that Telegram topics/routes are connection-scoped temporary views and should be cleaned up after disconnect, process close, crash/death, or expired automatic reconnect grace.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Inspection confirms current planning no longer treats session shutdown/death as a reason to preserve Telegram routes indefinitely; replacement lifecycle requirements define cleanup plus a bounded automatic reconnect exception.

requirements.label: List and select sessions
requirements.statement: The system shall expose Telegram commands that let the paired user list connected sessions and select the session that receives subsequent unrouted messages.
requirements.rationale: Multi-session supervision requires the operator to distinguish sessions and avoid sending work to an ambiguous target.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: A Telegram workflow demonstrates that /sessions shows available sessions with useful status and /use or equivalent selection makes later ordinary messages route to the chosen session.

requirements.label: Preserve session route context
requirements.statement: The system shall preserve per-session Telegram topic or route identifiers so messages, previews, activity, uploads, and final responses return to the correct session context.
requirements.rationale: Session identity must survive across the different Telegram methods used to show progress, send files, and deliver final answers.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show all routed send paths use the selected route and message_thread_id where applicable, and concurrent sessions do not cross-post activity or finals into each other's routes.

requirements.label: Deliver Telegram input to pi
requirements.statement: The system shall convert authorized Telegram text and prepared attachments into a pi user turn for the selected session when that session is available.
requirements.rationale: Telegram is the remote input surface, but execution remains in pi; inbound messages must become normal local session input rather than a separate Telegram-side runtime.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: A delivery test demonstrates that an authorized Telegram message reaches the selected pi session with expected text and attachment metadata while tool execution still occurs in the local pi session.

requirements.label: Steer active turn by default
requirements.statement: The system shall deliver an ordinary authorized Telegram message sent while the selected pi session is busy as steering for the active turn.
requirements.rationale: When the user speaks during an active run, the default remote-control intent is usually to correct or guide the current work.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: A busy-session test demonstrates that an ordinary Telegram message is sent to pi with steering delivery semantics and is not persisted for later duplicate retry as a normal queued turn.

requirements.label: Queue explicit follow-up
requirements.statement: The system shall deliver an authorized /follow message as follow-up work queued after the active turn instead of steering the active turn.
requirements.rationale: The operator needs an explicit way to distinguish next-step work from active-turn steering.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: A busy-session test demonstrates that /follow text is delivered with follow-up semantics, runs after the active turn, and is not mixed into current-turn steering.

requirements.label: Stop active turn remotely
requirements.statement: The system shall let the paired Telegram user abort the selected session's active turn with a stop command.
requirements.rationale: Remote babysitting requires a safe escape hatch when the active agent run is wrong, expensive, or no longer desired.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: A stop workflow demonstrates that /stop or stop aborts the selected active turn, stops associated activity indicators, and sends a single clear confirmation to Telegram.

requirements.label: Mirror turn after mid-connect
requirements.statement: The system shall start mirroring current activity and final response delivery to Telegram when /telegram-connect is invoked during an already-running pi turn.
requirements.rationale: The user may decide to leave after a turn has already started; connecting then must attach to the current run rather than only future runs.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: A mid-turn connection test demonstrates that current activity becomes visible in Telegram after connection and that the active turn's final response is delivered to Telegram.

requirements.label: Preserve activity while debouncing
requirements.statement: The system shall preserve ordered thinking and tool activity history while throttling Telegram message sends and edits used to render that activity.
requirements.rationale: Debouncing is necessary for Telegram limits, but the remote operator still needs the meaningful sequence of activity events.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests show multiple activity updates are retained in order, thinking updates do not rewrite older completed history, and Telegram edit/send operations are debounced without dropping collected events.

requirements.label: Send finals after previews
requirements.statement: The system shall detach any streamed assistant preview before final delivery and send final assistant response text as new Telegram messages rather than editing an older preview message into the final response.
requirements.rationale: Remote supervision depends on Telegram chronology as well as deduplication. Editing an earlier preview into the final answer can make the final appear before later Activity messages even when final delivery closes activity first; detaching the preview and appending fresh final messages keeps the visible result understandable while preserving duplicate-prevention goals. Preview cleanup should occur when Telegram permits it, but final answer text must not be delivered by reusing the preview message.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests show that when a visible preview exists, final delivery detaches it before sending final text with sendMessage; final content is not produced by editMessageText on the preview; missing/already-deleted previews still allow the final to be sent once; retry_after or ambiguous transport/server failures during preview cleanup keep the final pending without sending or bypassing FIFO ordering; permanent preview-cleanup failures still lead to final assistant text being appended with sendMessage rather than terminally losing the final or editing it into the preview; chunked final text and attachments are delivered once in order with route/thread context preserved.

requirements.label: Retry finals until delivered
requirements.statement: The system shall keep assistant final responses retryable in FIFO order until each final is successfully delivered to Telegram or reaches an explicit non-retryable terminal outcome.
requirements.rationale: Final answers are the user's durable outcome; broker acceptance alone is not enough if the broker can crash, turn over, or hit Telegram retry_after before successful Telegram delivery.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show pending finals remain queued across client-to-broker handoff, broker retry_after, and broker turnover until Telegram delivery succeeds or a clearly non-retryable terminal failure is recorded, and older queued finals are retried before newer finals.

requirements.label: Advance offsets after durable handling
requirements.statement: The system shall advance Telegram update offsets only after each update is durably handled, rejected, or queued for retry-safe delivery.
requirements.rationale: Telegram offset acknowledgement is the delivery safety boundary for long polling.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show lastProcessedUpdateId is persisted after durable handling decisions, duplicate update IDs are ignored safely, and first-start negative-offset initialization is not repeated indefinitely.

requirements.label: Honor Telegram retry_after
requirements.statement: The system shall honor Telegram retry_after signals for polling, JSON requests, uploads, downloads, preview updates, media preparation, and final delivery.
requirements.rationale: Rate-limit handling is a cross-cutting Telegram API constraint; treating it as an ordinary failure can duplicate, drop, or reorder user-visible work.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show TelegramApiError retry_after values are propagated and callers wait instead of falling back, reprocessing immediately, or bypassing older queued work.

requirements.label: Clear webhook before polling
requirements.statement: The system shall remove any configured Telegram webhook before using getUpdates polling and shall retry webhook removal failures before entering the polling loop.
requirements.rationale: Telegram does not allow getUpdates while a webhook is active, and this project intentionally uses polling instead of an inbound webhook endpoint.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show deleteWebhook is called with drop_pending_updates false before polling, transient deletion failures are retried, and getUpdates is not attempted while webhook removal is still failing.

requirements.label: Batch albums without loss
requirements.statement: The system shall batch Telegram media-group updates into one prepared turn and preserve late or retryable album updates for later processing.
requirements.rationale: Telegram albums arrive as multiple updates; losing late updates or retryable failures would produce incomplete context for the remote session.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests cover album debounce, processing snapshots, late updates during flush, and retry_after during file preparation, confirming unprocessed album updates remain pending and are rescheduled.

requirements.label: Download files privately within limits
requirements.statement: The system shall download Telegram files only within configured Bot API size limits and store downloaded files in private local directories and files.
requirements.rationale: Inbound Telegram files can be private or hostile; the bridge must respect hosted Bot API limits and local privacy expectations.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show file_size, getFile metadata, Content-Length, and streaming byte counts enforce the download cap; missing file_path fails clearly; temp directories use private permissions; and downloaded files are not world-readable.

requirements.label: Restrict outbound attachments
requirements.statement: The system shall send outbound attachments only from allowed local paths and shall block obvious secrets or unrelated local files from being uploaded to Telegram.
requirements.rationale: The telegram_attach path crosses from local workspace authority into Telegram and must not expose arbitrary local files.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show relative paths resolve against the session workspace, allowed paths are bounded to the workspace or bridge temp area, and blocked names such as .env, SSH keys, and cloud credential directories are not uploaded.

requirements.label: Respect photo document limits
requirements.statement: The system shall choose Telegram sendPhoto only for likely photos within Telegram photo constraints and shall use sendDocument for non-photo files or photo-contract fallback cases.
requirements.rationale: Telegram media methods have different constraints; wrong method selection causes avoidable delivery failures or rate-limit mishandling.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show images over the photo size cap use sendDocument, photo-specific rejections can fall back to sendDocument, and retry_after from sendPhoto is preserved rather than treated as fallback eligibility.

requirements.label: Keep execution authority local
requirements.statement: The system shall keep tool execution, workspace mutation, shell access, credentials, model selection, and session state inside the local pi session rather than transferring those authorities to Telegram.
requirements.rationale: The project owner explicitly wants everything to continue running on the laptop while Telegram acts as the control surface.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Inspection confirms Telegram handlers create pi inputs, commands, and attachment deliveries but do not provide independent shell execution, workspace mutation, credential access, or model execution outside the local pi runtime.

requirements.label: Maintain Telegram API guidance
requirements.statement: The repository shall maintain project-local Telegram Bot API guidance and agent instructions that identify the API constraints relevant to bridge changes.
requirements.rationale: Future maintainers need durable source-backed guidance for Telegram limits, retry behavior, polling, drafts, topics, and file handling instead of relying on compacted chat history.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Inspection confirms docs.md links to official Telegram sources for relevant Bot API behavior and AGENTS.md directs coding agents to consult those notes before Telegram integration changes.

requirements.label: Provide local validation command
requirements.statement: The repository shall provide a local validation command that type-checks the extension package before changes are reported complete.
requirements.rationale: Maintainers and coding agents need a repeatable sanity check for a TypeScript extension that spans broker, pi, shared, and Telegram modules.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Inspection confirms package scripts expose npm run check, the command runs TypeScript typechecking for the package, and AGENTS.md names it as required validation.

requirements.label: Return explicitly attached artifacts
requirements.statement: The system shall send local artifacts back to Telegram only when pi explicitly queues them for Telegram attachment, and shall attempt those queued uploads with the associated assistant reply.
requirements.rationale: Outbound artifact exchange is useful only when pi intentionally attaches generated files; Telegram should not be able to request arbitrary upload authority outside that explicit pi action.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show telegram_attach or the equivalent pi attachment path creates the outbound attachment queue, Telegram-originated messages cannot directly trigger arbitrary local uploads, and queued attachments are sent or reported as failed with the assistant reply.

requirements.label: Obey Telegram text contracts
requirements.statement: The system shall obey Telegram text method contracts for message sends, edits, and drafts, including non-empty text, 4096-character chunking, and draft eligibility constraints.
requirements.rationale: Previews and finals depend on Telegram methods with strict text and draft constraints; violating those contracts breaks remote visibility even when the logical response exists.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show sendMessage and editMessageText receive non-empty text below Telegram's limit, long finals are chunked below the limit, sendMessageDraft is used only for eligible integer private-chat targets with non-zero draft IDs, and rate limits are not treated as parse-mode or draft-contract failures.

requirements.label: Keep bridge secrets private
requirements.statement: The system shall keep bot tokens, local credentials, bridge configuration, broker state, IPC sockets, and downloaded Telegram data private and out of routine Telegram replies or logs.
requirements.rationale: A paired Telegram bridge controls powerful local sessions; leaking tokens, socket paths, credentials, or broker state would weaken both authorization and local-first trust.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Inspection confirms persisted config/state and IPC surfaces use private local storage where applicable, bot tokens and credentials are not logged or echoed to Telegram in normal operation, and downloaded Telegram files are stored with private permissions.

requirements.label: Treat inbound attachments untrusted
requirements.statement: The system shall treat Telegram attachment filenames, MIME types, metadata, paths, and contents as untrusted input when preparing local files for pi.
requirements.rationale: Telegram attachments cross a trust boundary; private storage alone does not prevent hostile filenames, misleading MIME types, or metadata from influencing local behavior.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show inbound filenames are sanitized or bounded before local use, metadata is preserved only as descriptive context, attachment contents are not executed by the bridge, and generated prompts treat attachment paths as user-provided files rather than trusted instructions.

requirements.label: Preserve selector session choice
requirements.statement: The system shall preserve selector-mode session selection across broker turnover until the selection expires, is changed, or becomes invalid.
requirements.rationale: Selector mode routes subsequent unrouted Telegram messages through the user's /use choice; losing that choice during broker takeover breaks routing continuity even though session routes still exist.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show the selected session for a selector-mode chat is represented in durable broker-scoped state, survives broker takeover within its expiry window, is cleared or rejected when the selected session becomes invalid, and can be changed by a later /use command.

requirements.label: Preserve reload intent
requirements.statement: The system shall preserve a Telegram reload intent across the reload boundary until the reloaded session either reports reattachment status to Telegram or records that reporting is no longer possible.
requirements.rationale: Deprecated 2026-04-25: Durable Telegram reload intents are removed along with the unsupported Telegram reload command.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Inspection confirms reload intent state and retry/reporting plumbing for Telegram-triggered runtime reload are absent or ignored as legacy persisted state.

requirements.label: Route Telegram reload command
requirements.statement: The system shall treat an authorized Telegram /reload command for a routed or selected session as a pi runtime reload request for that session rather than as an agent turn.
requirements.rationale: Deprecated 2026-04-25: The Telegram /reload command is removed because it cannot be implemented safely with the current public pi extension API and must not be surfaced as a normal agent turn.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Inspection confirms /reload is not advertised or specially handled by Telegram command routing, and no internal /telegram-reload-runtime command is injected as user content.

requirements.label: Reattach route after reload
requirements.statement: After a Telegram-triggered reload, the system shall reconnect the reloaded pi session to the same valid Telegram route that initiated the reload.
requirements.rationale: Deprecated 2026-04-25: Route reattachment after Telegram-triggered reload is removed because Telegram-triggered reload itself is unsupported.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Inspection confirms session registration and routing no longer consume Telegram reload intents or report Telegram reload completion.

requirements.label: Mirror local input clearly
requirements.statement: The system shall mirror local interactive pi user messages to the connected Telegram route with formatting that clearly identifies them as local pi user input.
requirements.rationale: Remote supervision is clearer when Telegram shows not only assistant progress and finals but also local user input that happened in the connected pi session, without making those mirrored messages look like unstructured assistant output.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: A test or inspection demonstrates that a non-command interactive pi input on a connected route sends one Telegram message to the same route/thread with a distinct local-user title, preserves the message content and image-count notice, and still ignores slash commands or Telegram prompt text.

requirements.label: Compact busy sessions remotely
requirements.statement: The system shall invoke pi manual compaction for an authorized Telegram /compact command on the selected session so that pi interrupts any active turn before compacting according to native manual compaction semantics.
requirements.rationale: Native pi treats /compact as a control command that interrupts an active turn and compacts; Telegram control should preserve the same remote-session expectation instead of rejecting busy sessions.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: A focused test shows that /compact sent to a busy selected session invokes pi manual compaction, interrupts the active turn through that path, and produces the normal compaction completion or failure reply rather than the busy-session rejection, while ordinary busy Telegram messages still steer and /follow still queues.

requirements.label: Clean route on close
requirements.statement: The system shall unregister a connected pi session and remove its Telegram route and topic when the session explicitly disconnects or emits a normal session shutdown.
requirements.rationale: A deliberate disconnect or process close ends the Telegram view; unrelated sessions must remain connected and local pi history remains resumable outside Telegram.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests show /telegram-disconnect, Telegram /disconnect, and a normal pi session_shutdown remove only the target session route, request deleteForumTopic for topic routes, stop target typing/turn state, and leave unrelated sessions/routes reachable.

requirements.label: Clean after reconnect grace
requirements.statement: The system shall preserve a connected session's Telegram route only during a bounded automatic reconnect grace period after heartbeat or IPC loss, and shall unregister the session and remove its Telegram route and topic when that grace expires without successful reconnect.
requirements.rationale: Slow network, broker turnover, or brief local IPC gaps may be recoverable, but a crashed or dead session must not leave a permanent Telegram topic behind.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests simulate missed heartbeats or IPC failure: reconnect before the grace expires reuses the existing route without deleting its topic, while no reconnect by expiry removes the route/topic and later /resume plus /telegram-connect creates a fresh route/topic.

requirements.label: Retry topic cleanup
requirements.statement: The system shall keep Telegram topic cleanup retryable until topic deletion succeeds or reaches an explicit non-retryable terminal outcome.
requirements.rationale: Removing local route state while deleteForumTopic is rate-limited or transiently failing can orphan the temporary Telegram view the user expects to disappear.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show deleteForumTopic retry_after and transient failures leave cleanup pending for retry, terminal not-found/already-deleted outcomes complete cleanup idempotently, and permission/auth terminal failures are surfaced without blocking unrelated session cleanup.

requirements.label: Defer Telegram during compaction
requirements.statement: The system shall defer ordinary authorized Telegram messages and authorized /follow work received while the selected pi session is running manual compaction started by an authorized Telegram /compact command, shall not start a concurrent pi turn until that compaction ends, and shall resume that deferred input afterward with the first deferred input item starting the next turn, later deferred ordinary messages steering that turn, and later deferred /follow work remaining queued behind it.
requirements.rationale: Native pi queues user input while manual compaction is running and flushes it only after compaction ends. Telegram supervision must preserve that same local-session control model instead of starting a second turn while pi is still compacting.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: A focused regression shows that while Telegram-started manual compaction is running, ordinary Telegram messages and explicit /follow work are held instead of starting a turn; once compaction finishes, the first deferred input item, including a lone deferred /follow, starts the next turn, later deferred ordinary messages steer that turn, later deferred /follow work stays queued behind it, and no concurrent turn starts before compaction ends.

requirements.label: Silence passive Telegram updates
requirements.statement: The system shall set disabled notifications on Telegram sendMessage calls for broker activity-renderer status messages and mirrored local pi user input while preserving ordinary notification behavior for assistant previews, final answers, and explicit operator-facing command or error replies.
requirements.rationale: Remote supervision should keep the Telegram view current without alerting the operator for passive broker activity rendering or echoes of local input they just typed on the computer. Telegram provides disable_notification on sendMessage, so this behavior can be checked at the bridge send boundary without changing route identity, assistant preview/final semantics, or command/error reply notification behavior.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or inspection show all targeted broker activity-renderer sendMessage calls and local-user mirror sendMessage calls include disable_notification=true for every chunk and preserve message_thread_id, while assistant previews, final assistant replies, setup/command replies, explicit errors, attachment failure notices, and rate-limit handling are not made silent unless separately required.

requirements.label: Defer transient final errors
requirements.statement: The system shall defer Telegram finalization for an active Telegram turn when pi reports a retryable transient assistant or provider error without non-empty assistant final text, and shall deliver the subsequent stable assistant final response or a clear terminal failure outcome instead of the transient error string.
requirements.rationale: A remote operator expects Telegram to reflect the stable outcome of the pi turn. Pi can emit an agent_end for a transient provider error before its own auto-retry produces a successful local final; treating that transient event as the Telegram final loses the later answer and breaks continuity.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests simulate an active Telegram turn whose first agent_end contains retryable errors such as fetch failed or terminated and no assistant final text: no assistant_final is sent, the Telegram turn remains associated with the retry, stale previews are cleared or safely superseded, a later successful final is delivered to Telegram, and an exhausted or non-retried transient error produces one clear terminal failure without blocking /stop or queued turns. Payloads that include non-empty assistant final text are handled by SyRS-final-text-before-error-metadata instead of being deferred solely because error metadata is present.

requirements.label: Prefer final text to errors
requirements.statement: The system shall use non-empty assistant final text as the Telegram final response before using assistant stop or error metadata to construct a clear failure response when no assistant text is available.
requirements.rationale: Telegram finals should preserve useful assistant output. Raw provider or stop metadata such as fetch failed or terminated is useful diagnostic context but should not replace non-empty final text that the local pi session can show.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests cover stopReason=error with non-empty text and low-context errorMessage values, confirming Telegram receives the assistant text rather than only the raw error; error-only finals construct a clear failure response from stop/error metadata instead of exposing low-context raw strings as the whole final; aborted turns continue to clear previews without creating misleading final text; and Telegram API send failures remain delivery failures rather than message content.

requirements.label: Pick exact model interactively
requirements.statement: The system shall let the paired Telegram user change the selected pi session's model through interactive Telegram controls that preserve the exact local provider/model identity, including subscription-provider grouping when multiple providers expose the same model IDs.
requirements.rationale: Remote model switching is part of using Telegram as a control surface for local pi sessions, but provider identity must stay local and exact because optional subscription extensions can expose the same model ID through multiple provider accounts.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Tests or a focused demonstration show that bare /model presents an interactive picker; catalogs with multiple provider/subscription groups that expose overlapping model IDs first present provider/subscription choices and then scoped model choices; single-group catalogs go directly to model choices; pagination keeps selections usable when a page would exceed the button budget; callback handling authorizes the paired user/chat, rejects expired or mismatched route/session state without changing models, and sends the exact provider/id to the target session; existing /model <number> and /model <selector> compatibility remains intact.
